description: Design named-account support: agents permanently or primarily tied to account sets (service account manager style), with a model for commercial pods and large single-segment pools.

examples.context: Commercial accounts needed continuity. Built agent sets wholly tied to account groups.
examples.resolution: Named ownership. Sales knew who to pull. Repeat context down.

examples.context: Large institutional segment behaved like one relationship; dedicated group owned it.
examples.resolution: One pool, clear intake. Treated as named structure not random queue.

examples.context: One agent held all tribal knowledge for top accounts.
examples.resolution: Bench trained; notes living. Vacation no longer an outage.
